content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                          Date : April 30, 1980


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Dwight Hickerson

Subject: Disclosure of Collateral used for Loans

     It was decided by staff at the Opinion request
meeting of April 24, 1980, that there must be detailed
disclosure of collateral used by an official to obtain
a reportable loan. The disclosure is sufficiently
detailed if a person reviewing the statement can
specifically identify the collateral used by the
official.

DD:plh

State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants                    Date : April 22, 1980


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Dwight Dickerson

Subject: Disclosure of collateral used for loans

       Government Code Section 87207(a)(5) requires that
the security, if any, given for a reportable loan be
disclosed. With respect to an interest in real property
used as collateral, how detailed must the description
be? Can the collateral be described as real property or
must the property be described by lot and parcel number?
If an official uses a deed of trust as collateral for a
reportable loan, must the real property be identified?

       The instruction manual for the Form 730 provides an
example that describes the collateral for a reportable
loan, which I have attached.

DD:Plb
Attachment
